architecture_summary: The paper proposes large kernel attention (LKA), a novel linear attention mechanism that decomposes a large-kernel convolution into depth-wise convolution, depth-wise dilation convolution, and 1×1 convolution. Based on LKA, it builds Visual Attention Network (VAN), a simple hierarchical vision backbone with four stages and seven variants from VAN-B0 to VAN-B6.

novel_contributions: We design a novel linear attention mechanism named LKA for computer vision, which considers the pros of both convolution and self-attention, while avoiding their cons.
Based on LKA, we further introduce a simple vision backbone called VAN.

We show that VANs achieve comparable results with similar level CNNs and ViTs in extensive experiments on various tasks, including image classification, object detection, semantic segmentation, instance segmentation, pose estimation, etc.

key_claims.claim: LKA is a novel linear attention mechanism tailored for visual tasks that combines local structure information, long-range dependence, and adaptability while avoiding the shortcomings of standard self-attention.
VAN is a simple hierarchical vision backbone built from LKA and four stages with decreasing output spatial resolution.
VAN-B6 achieves 87.8% accuracy on ImageNet benchmark and VAN-B6 sets new state-of-the-art performance for panoptic segmentation with 58.2% PQ.
VAN-B2 surpasses Swin-T by 4% mIoU on ADE20K and by 2.6% AP on COCO in the abstract.
On ImageNet-1K, VAN-B2 outperforms Swin-T and ConvNeXt-T in the comparison discussed in Section 4.1.1.
On COCO panoptic segmentation, VAN-B6* achieves 58.2% PQ and is explicitly described as new state-of-the-art.
On ADE20K, VAN-B5 and VAN-B6 achieve 53.9% and 54.7% mIoU respectively under ImageNet-22K pretraining.
